# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an IAM role that allows cross-account access for our CI/CD system. Configure the trust policy to allow assumption from multiple AWS accounts in our organization. Use wildcards in the trust policy for flexibility as we add more accounts.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: IAM Role for cross-account CI/CD access with a flexible trust policy for multiple AWS accounts.

Parameters:
  TrustedAccountIds:
    Type: CommaDelimitedList
    Description: Comma-separated list of AWS account IDs that are allowed to assume this role.
    Default: '111122223333,444455556666'

Resources:
  CiCdCrossAccountRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: CiCdCrossAccountRole
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Split [',', !Ref TrustedAccountIds]
            Action:
              - sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/ReadOnlyAccess
      Description: IAM Role to be assumed by CI/CD systems from specified trusted accounts for cross-account operations.

Outputs:
  CiCdCrossAccountRoleArn:
    Description: The ARN of the CI/CD Cross-Account Role
    Value: !GetAtt CiCdCrossAccountRole.Arn
